title: Make first adoption installation transactional
outOfScope: Adopter release manifest boundary reserved for the next Work Item
External review ownership and platform rulesets

sources.reason: Task 6 acceptance and P1-3 review mapping.
Current adoption preflight, branch mutation, installation, and rollback implementation.
Existing installer conflict and rollback regression coverage.
Existing first-adoption and remote-base end-to-end coverage.
Installer behavior and adoption lifecycle documentation.
Installed adoption workflow and readiness boundary.

problemStatement: First adoption can mutate the adopter branch before marker and managed-conflict validation, dry-run can still fetch or create a branch, and rollback does not restore the exact original Git HEAD state.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Dry-run adoption performs no remote sync, branch creation, switch, or deletion.
Marker and managed-conflict failures happen before branch mutation.
Runtime failure restores the original branch or detached HEAD and filesystem exactly.
Successful adoption creates the expected branch and records the correct base.

riskAssessment.reason: The installer changes an adopter repository's Git state and governance files; an incomplete rollback can strand users on the wrong branch or leave partial adoption files.

executionDecision.reason: The required behavior is specified by the review plan and current implementation has deterministic testable mutation boundaries.

preReviewWarnings: Preserve non-branch Git states where supported; do not use destructive reset or checkout as rollback.
Dry-run must remain read-only, including Git network and reference operations.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record Git-state transaction boundaries before implementation and finish.

acceptance: --dry-run --create-adoption remains Git read-only with no remote sync, branch creation, switch, or deletion.
Managed conflict and Agent Marker validation complete before any branch mutation.
Rollback restores the exact original branch/HEAD state, including detached HEAD where supported.
Focused E2E coverage verifies dry-run, marker failure, managed-conflict failure, runtime failure, and successful adoption.
guidelines: Keep adoption preflight read-only and complete all validation before repository mutation.
Treat branch and filesystem changes as one transaction with explicit restoration evidence.
Document the actual installer boundary without claiming external review or release readiness.

intent.problem: A first-adoption install can change Git state before all validations pass and its rollback can leave the adopter detached from the original branch.
constraints: Do not weaken managed conflict or marker validation.
Do not make dry-run perform network or Git mutation.
Preserve the existing successful adoption workflow and audit records.
intent.rationale: A transactional adoption boundary prevents partial governance installation and makes failed onboarding recoverable without manual Git repair.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User confirmed continuation of Task 6 within the declared scope, including restricted paths .ai/cockpit/adoption.md and .ai/guards/governance_complexity_policy.yaml.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
